directive.title: RFQ status transition API with audit log wiring

auditor.summary: The generated code has a critical inconsistency between the migration file and schema definition - the migration creates a column named 'status_enum' while the schema expects 'status'. Additionally, the migration contains prohibited UPDATE statements. The audit_logs schema has a TypeScript compilation error due to missing 'sql' import. The codebase is missing a database client initialization file (lib/db.ts) that both API routes depend on. The state machine logic and unit tests are well-implemented and cover all required transition paths. The PATCH endpoint correctly implements atomic transactions with audit logging. However, the critical migration and compilation issues prevent this build from passing. Score is 55 due to critical migration inconsistency and compilation errors.

top_issues.description: Migration adds column named 'status_enum' instead of 'status' as specified in schema and directive. Also contains UPDATE statement with CASE logic, violating the 'ADD COLUMN only' constraint. Schema file db/schema/rfqs.ts defines column as 'status' but migration creates 'status_enum' - they are inconsistent.

Uses 'sql' template literal tag without importing it from drizzle-orm. The check() function usage with sql`...` requires import { sql } from 'drizzle-orm'. This will cause TypeScript compilation error.
The check() function from drizzle-orm/pg-core may not accept the signature used here. The event_type_check constraint definition may not be valid Drizzle syntax for CHECK constraints.
